---
output: pdf_document
---
# DeepSeek V3 0324 vs OpenAI: GPT-4o-mini
```{r prompt-histograms-individual, echo=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# Load necessary package
library(ggplot2)
library(pwr)

# 15 Branchen, mittlerer Effekt, 0.05 Signifikanzniveau, 0.8 Power
pwr.chisq.test(w = 0.3, df = 14, sig.level = 0.05, power = 0.8)

# Read the CSV file
data1 <- read.csv("industry_responses_500DS.csv")
data2 <- read.csv("industry_responses_500_openai_gpt-4o-mini.csv")
data3 <- read.csv("industry_responses_500_x-ai_grok-3-mini-beta.csv")


categories <- c(
  "Bau, Baunebengewerbe, Holz, Gebäudetechnik",
        "Bergbau, Rohstoffe, Glas, Keramik, Stein",
        "Büro, Marketing, Finanz, Recht, Sicherheit",
        "Chemie, Biotechnologie, Lebensmittel, Kunststoffe",
        "Elektrotechnik, Elektronik, Telekommunikation, IT",
        "Handel, Logistik, Verkehr",
        "Landwirtschaft, Gartenbau, Forstwirtschaft",
        "Maschinenbau, Kfz, Metall",
        "Medien, Grafik, Design, Druck, Kunst, Kunsthandwerk",
        "Reinigung, Hausbetreuung, Anlern- und Hilfsberufe",
        "Soziales, Gesundheit, Schönheitspflege",
        "Textil und Bekleidung, Mode, Leder",
        "Tourismus, Gastgewerbe, Freizeit",
        "Umwelt",
        "Wissenschaft, Bildung, Forschung und Entwicklung"
)


#Count the amount of industries
observed1 <- table(factor(data1$industry, levels = categories))
observed2 <- table(factor(data2$industry, levels = categories))
observed3 <- table(factor(data3$industry, levels = categories))

#Expected distribution of the data
expected_probs <- rep(1 / length(categories), length(categories))

#Chi Squared Test
chisq.test(x = observed1, p = expected_probs)
chisq.test(x = observed2, p = expected_probs)
chisq.test(x = observed3, p = expected_probs)

# Cramers V for observed1
X1 <- 5880.4
df1 <- 14
k1 <- df1 + 1
N1 <- sum(observed1)
V1 <- sqrt(X1 / (N1 * (k1 - 1)))

print(V1)

# For Cramers V observed2
X2 <- 2780
df2 <- 14
k2 <- df2 + 1
N2 <- sum(observed2)
V2 <- sqrt(X2 / (N2 * (k2 - 1)))
print(V2)

# For Cramers V observed3
X3 <- 4943.4
df3 <- 14
k3 <- df3 + 1
N3 <- sum(observed3)
V3 <- sqrt(X3 / (N3 * (k3 - 1)))
print(V3)

# Create a bar chart of industry frequencies
ggplot(data1, aes(x = industry)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Number of Requests per Industry (DeepSeek)",
       x = "Faculty",
       y = "Number of Requests") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Create a bar chart of industry frequencies
ggplot(data2, aes(x = industry)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Number of Requests per Industry (ChatGPT)",
       x = "Faculty",
       y = "Number of Requests") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

# Create a bar chart of industry frequencies
ggplot(data3, aes(x = industry)) +
  geom_bar(fill = "skyblue", color = "black") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Number of Requests per Industry (Grok)",
       x = "Faculty",
       y = "Number of Requests") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```

</div></pre>
